trigger.reason: OpenAI's reasoning model solving a major 80-year-old math conjecture is a high-impact capability demonstration from a top-tier lab that will draw broad market attention and trigger sympathy-fade selling across AI-adjacent sectors (SaaS productivity tools, low-code platforms, customer-service software).

primary_event.headline: OpenAI claims it solved an 80-year-old math problem — for real this time
primary_event.summary: OpenAI's reasoning model disproved a geometry conjecture unsolved since 1946, with independent verification from mathematicians who had previously caught OpenAI making false claims. This represents a major capability milestone for AI reasoning and problem-solving.

secondary_events.headline: Google just declared itself a contender in AI design at IO 2026
Google is launching AI-powered information agents that monitor topics and proactively alert users
Stability AI releases a new audio model that can create 6-minute songs

discovery.trigger_acknowledgment: Analyzed OpenAI's claim of solving an 80-year-old geometry conjecture via its reasoning model — a capability demo triggering broad AI-adjacent sympathy moves.
discovery.run_summary: The trigger is a reasoning/research capability milestone, which most directly threatens edtech, research software, and knowledge-worker SaaS — not semis or HSA custodians. Today's basket is dominated by names moving UP on the news (semis-pick-shovels, edge-AI, cyber-data-governance — all AI capex/deployment beneficiaries), so the OVERDONE-on-downside setup applies only to names that sold off. DUOL is the cleanest sympathy-fade short-fuse candidate; PHR also sold off but the threat read is more nuanced.

discoveries.name: Duolingo, Inc.
discoveries.filings_evidence: 10-K explicitly names GenAI as a competitive risk: 'a new product could gain rapid scale at the expense of existing brands through harnessing a new technology (such as generative artificial intelligence)' and notes 'thousands of free mobile applications for language learning.' But DUOL has also launched Duolingo Max powered by GenAI, indicating it is integrating rather than purely being disrupted by the technology.
discoveries.thesis: DUOL is the reflexive edtech sell on any OpenAI capability headline, but today's trigger is a math-reasoning demo, not a consumer language-learning product. DUOL's moat is the daily-habit dopamine/streak loop and gamification, not instructional content quality — areas a reasoning model breakthrough does not directly attack. The selloff is partial overshoot relative to the actual threat substance.
discoveries.what_confirms: Stable or rising DAU/MAU and paid subscriber metrics in the next quarterly print; continued growth of Duolingo Max attach; no acceleration of churn following the news cycle.
discoveries.what_kills: Evidence that an OpenAI consumer-facing tutor product is shipping with language-learning features; meaningful DAU decline in the next print; Apple/Google App Store featuring competing AI tutors prominently.
discoveries.what_to_learn: Edtech consumer apps (especially DUOL) sell off on essentially every OpenAI release regardless of whether the substance is consumer-facing or domain-relevant — track these reactions over time to calibrate the habitual overshoot magnitude.
discoveries.catalyst: OpenAI claims it solved an 80-year-old math problem — for real this time
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: DUOL is the canonical retail sympathy-sell on any OpenAI capability news, and the trigger detector flagged consumer SaaS/productivity as at-risk.
research_pointers: Track DUOL DAU trend through next earnings — has the GPT-5/reasoning era actually dented engagement?
Compare DUOL Max attach rate trajectory vs. analyst consumer-AI-displacement assumptions
Measure historical 1-day and 5-day post-OpenAI-event move on DUOL to estimate fade probability

Did sell off, but filings show real and explicit AI-as-competitor language ('AI technologies may make it easier for competitors to enter our market due to lower up-front costs'); threat is indirect-to-direct on intake workflows, panic calibration is closer to justified than unjustified — not a clean OVERDONE call
Moved UP — HSA custodian moat (trust-bank regulation) intact, no downside panic to fade

discovery.no_signals_note: The trigger drove most AI-adjacent semis and infrastructure names UP (capex/deployment beneficiaries), leaving only edtech (DUOL) and healthcare-workflow (PHR) as downside candidates. PHR's filings reveal genuine AI exposure that disqualifies it as a clean false-victim; DUOL is the lone OVERDONE call, and only at moderate confidence given the magnitude of move and that DUOL's own filings name GenAI as a material competitive risk.
